event: Pompeji Have (House of the Chirurgus with the Vesuv)
symbols: ruined columns — the transience of human civilization and the vanity of earthly splendor
Vesuvius — divine judgment and the sudden destruction of the wicked
empty courtyard — desolation after catastrophic judgment
impluvium basin — domestic life interrupted by cataclysm
description: A painting of the interior courtyard of a Roman house in Pompeii, viewed through standing columns. Several tall reddish-brown and pale stone columns frame the scene, supporting fragments of entablature. In the center foreground lies a shallow rectangular impluvium basin set into the stone floor. Beyond the columns, the ruins of additional structures are visible, with Mount Vesuvius rising prominently in the background, its summit partly shrouded in atmospheric haze. The palette is warm, with ochres, burnt sienna, and muted greens under a pale sky.

_meta.title: Pompeji Have (House of the Chirurgus with the Vesuv)
